items.title: ChatLoopBackOff: Episode 65 (Kubean)
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content: <article>Ready to simplify Kubernetes cluster management across hybrid or multi-cloud environments? In this Live demo, CNCF Ambassador Afzal Ansari explores Kubean, a CNCF project that brings an Ansible-powered, GitOps-friendly approach to Kubernetes operations. Kubean aims to streamline the full lifecycle management of Kubernetes clusters—provisioning, upgrading, scaling, and decommissioning—using declarative configurations and a plugin-based architecture. Afzal will walk through the project’s core components, check out its integration with tools like Cluster API and OpenKruise, and assess its community momentum and production readiness. If you're interested in scalable cluster automation with a flexible control plane, don’t miss this first look at what Kubean has to offer.</article>

items.title: CNCF On demand webinar: Troubleshooting Kubernetes with confidence
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content: <article>Kubernetes debugging remains one of the most frustrating and time-consuming challenges in cloud native operations. When production issues hit, even seasoned platform engineers and SREs can find themselves grasping at logs, manually exec-ing into pods, and relying on shallow output from kubectl describe. In this conversational session, we sit down with Alban the creator of Traceloop and a core contributor to Inspektor Gadget, to explore how modern debugging is evolving, and what’s next. You’ll get a behind-the-scenes look at how Traceloop introduces kubectl-native tracing, powered by eBPF, to bring retroactive debugging into real-world Kubernetes clusters. Whether you're maintaining uptime, resolving incidents, or building internal tooling, this session will give you the practical insight and tools you need to troubleshoot Kubernetes with clarity and confidence.</article>

items.title: Cloud Native Live - Kyverno 1.15: New Policy Types
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Join us next week for a follow-up session where we explore the new policy types introduced in Kyverno 1.15 — MutatingPolicies, DeletingPolicies, and GeneratingPolicies.

This builds on our previous session, where we discussed Kyverno’s shift to CEL and introduced ValidatingPolicies and ImageValidatingPolicy. We’ll dive into how these new policies work, use cases they unlock, and how they fit into the future of Kubernetes policy management.</article>

items.title: Keynote: DX Is the New UX: How Technical Friction Impacts Business Value - Atulpriya Sharma
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon North America in Atlanta (November 10-13). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io

Keynote: DX Is the New UX: How Technical Friction Impacts Business Value - Atulpriya Sharma, Senior Developer Advocate, InfraCloud Technologies

Remember how you instantly close that glitchy food delivery app when it freezes mid-order? The same principle now applies to developer tools. Every minute spent battling deployment pipelines or debugging environment issues creates unnecessary friction.

This friction impacts your ability to deliver business value. It affects the things that matter: speed to market, talent retention, and competitive advantage. Leading organizations today are now designing for developer joy with the same intensity they apply to customer experiences.

Let us look at how forward-thinking technology leaders are transforming their platforms into competitive advantages by designing for developer delight. We’ll also learn how community insights can accelerate your DX improvements and why investing in seamless developer workflows is now a strategic priority that directly impacts your bottom line in today's talent-driven market.

Great DX isn't just a technical nicety—it's how you'll win in today's competitive market.</article>

items.title: Keynote: Pushing the Limits: Scaling ArgoCD’s Repo-Server To Handle 30... Aditya Sharma & Isha Gusai
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon India in Hyderabad (August 6-7), and KubeCon + CloudNativeCon North America in Atlanta (November 10-13). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io

Keynote: Pushing the Limits: Scaling ArgoCD’s Repo-Server To Handle 30K Applications - Aditya Sharma & Isha Gusai, Expedia Group

Expedia Group’s RCP platform is transitioning to ArgoCD as its control plane. As part of this journey, we are adapting ArgoCD’s industry-leading technology to meet our current and future needs. A critical component of this effort involves fine-tuning the repo-server, originally optimized for GitOps workflows, to manage over 4,000 unique Helm charts at a scale of 12,000 applications, with a forecasted future scale of 30,000 applications.

This talk delves into our deep-dive scalability testing of the repo-server component, exploring memory usage patterns, troubleshooting performance bottlenecks, and addressing memory burst issues. We share our solutions to stabilize the repo-server under heavy load, and provide actionable recommendations based on configurations and parameters that worked for us. These insights aim to benefit the broader community in their own scalability endeavors.</article>

items.title: Keynote: Kubernetes for GenAI: The New Operating System of the AI-First World - Janakiram MSV
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon North America in Atlanta (November 10-13). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io

Keynote: Kubernetes for GenAI: The New Operating System of the AI-First World - Janakiram MSV, Principal Analyst, Janakiram and Associates

As Generative AI moves from prototype to production, Kubernetes has emerged as the foundational substrate powering this transition. In this session, I explore how Kubernetes is accelerating the adoption of GenAI and AI agents—from orchestrating large model training and serving, to powering Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), fine-tuning pipelines, and real-time inference, and agentic workflows. I discuss real-world implementations, cutting-edge open-source projects, and reusable design patterns that highlight how the cloud-native ecosystem is evolving to meet the unique demands of AI-native workloads. Whether you’re building foundation models or deploying intelligent agents at scale, Kubernetes is fast becoming the operating system of the GenAI revolution.</article>

items.title: Keynote: Champions of Cloud Native: Stories and Insights from CNCF Ambassadors - Panel
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon North America in Atlanta (November 10-13). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io

Keynote: Champions of Cloud Native: Stories and Insights from CNCF Ambassadors - Monika Rajput, Ahead LLC; Divya Mohan, SUSE; Daniel Oh, Red Hat; Kimberly Falk, CNCF

The CNCF Ambassador Program brings together passionate community leaders who drive the cloud native movement forward through education, advocacy, and collaboration. In this keynote panel, we spotlight the voices behind the program, CNCF Ambassadors who have made meaningful contributions to the ecosystem.

Panelists will share their personal journeys: what inspired them to become Ambassadors, the challenges they’ve overcome, and the impact they’ve made. We’ll explore the tangible benefits of being part of the program, the knowledge they’ve gained, and how they continue to empower others in the community. Attendees will also hear first-hand advice for those aspiring to become future Ambassadors, and discover why these leaders play a vital role in the growth and success of the CNCF community worldwide.

Join us for an inspiring conversation that celebrates community, leadership, and the heart of the cloud native movement.</article>

items.title: Keynote: From Grass to Glass: Chitale Dairy's App Tra... Vishvas Parshuram Chitale & Dilpreet Bindra
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Don't miss out! Join us at our next Flagship Conference: KubeCon + CloudNativeCon North America in Atlanta (November 10-13). Connect with our current graduated, incubating, and sandbox projects as the community gathers to further the education and advancement of cloud native computing. Learn more at https://kubecon.io

Keynote: From Grass to Glass: Chitale Dairy's App Transformation with Kubernetes - Vishvas Parshuram Chitale, CEO Chitale Group & Dilpreet Bindra, Sr. Director, Engineering, Broadcom

Chitale Dairy digitized every aspect of operations by transitioning all software to modern containerized applications. Working with Sunfire, a Broadcom Advantage Partner, Chitale Dairy leveraged vSphere Kubernetes Service, a component in VMware Cloud Foundation, to accelerate their transformation. Infusing digital technology in every step of production the company also added a business-to-consumer delivery model at cloud speed.</article>

items.title: CNL: From Idea to Implementation - How to Self-Host an AI Agent
items.author: CNCF [Cloud Native Computing Foundation]
items.content:
<article>Generative and agentic AI are opening new opportunities to streamline business processes and improve automation. For enterprises in highly regulated environments, however, concerns around data privacy, security, and vendor reliance often require self-hosted solutions.

In this session, we’ll explore the open-source landscape and share use cases where hosting your own AI stack can support compliance and offer greater architectural flexibility. We’ll walk through the full development cycle—from choosing the right model and infrastructure to building and deploying a working AI agent.

By the end, you’ll gain practical insights to help you design and implement self-hosted GenAI applications tailored to your organization’s needs for control, customization, and cost management.</article>
